Name: Bucket Sort
Author: E. J. Isaac and R. C. Singleton
Description: Bucket sort, or bin sort, is a sorting algorithm that works by distributing the elements of an array into a number of buckets. Each bucket is then sorted individually, either using a different sorting algorithm or by recursively applying the bucket sorting algorithm. It is a distribution sort, a generalization of pigeonhole sort, and is most effective when the input is uniformly distributed over a range.
Algorithm: async function sort(){let r=Math.min(...array),a=Math.max(...array);const t=Math.floor((a-r)/array.length)+1,e=[];for(let r=0;r<t;r++)e.push([]);for(let a=0;a<array.length;a++){e[Math.floor((array[a]-r)/array.length)].push(array[a])}for(let r=0;r<e.length;r++){const a=e[r].sort(((r,a)=>r-a));for(let r=0;r<a.length;r++){await compare(r,array.indexOf(a[r]));if(swap(r,array.indexOf(a[r])),await sleep(),firstRun)return}}}

Code.Python:
def bucket_sort(arr):
	if len(arr) == 0:
		return arr
	min_val, max_val = min(arr), max(arr)
	bucket_count = len(arr)
	buckets = [[] for _ in range(bucket_count)]
	for num in arr:
		index = int((num - min_val) / (max_val - min_val + 1) * bucket_count)
		buckets[index].append(num)
	res = []
	for bucket in buckets:
		bucket.sort()
		res.extend(bucket)
	return res
